summary: Recent updates to the WebGPU area of Firefox focused on improving memory management by fixing issues related to the cycle collecting of final classes that use the _FINAL macro. WebGPU is a modern graphics API designed to bring high-performance graphics and computing to web applications, enabling developers to create more dynamic and visually rich experiences. By enhancing memory management, these changes help ensure more efficient use of system resources, which ultimately leads to smoother performance when running intensive graphics applications in Firefox. This is particularly important for applications that rely on fast rendering and processing, such as video games and interactive visualizations.
